core_mechanism: percolation / connectivity phase transition on a network under targeted edge removal, in which a sharp critical threshold causes the giant connected component to collapse from encompassing most nodes to a small fraction
brief_reason: The abstract's central structural result is that removing roads in order of conflict intensity reveals a tipping point at which the giant connected component drops from 85% to 39% of the network, which is a percolation/giant-component threshold under targeted removal (the Hawkes self-exciting kernel only supplies the intensity ranking that drives the removals).
